summary: Matched the lipstick item object by moving src/melee/it/items/itlipstick.c from NonMatching to Matching, adding the lipstick ItemStateTable definition in C, including the item forward declarations header needed for state callback references, and correcting one sdata2 float symbol from a global it_804DCB68 name to a local @158 label.
what_changed: In configure.py, changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itlipstick.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itlipstick.c"), marking the lipstick item as matching.
In src/melee/it/items/itlipstick.c, added #include "it/items/forward.h" before using item motion callback symbols.

In src/melee/it/items/itlipstick.c, added global ItemStateTable it_803F6310[] with six entries for lipstick animation, physics, and collision callbacks, including one NULL collision callback in the third entry.
In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, changed .sdata2:0x804DCB68 from global symbol it_804DCB68 to local symbol @158, preserving type object, size 0x4, align 4, data float.
smart_moves: The fix centers on data reconstruction rather than forcing code changes: adding the missing ItemStateTable likely resolved object layout and relocation expectations for the item file.
Using it/items/forward.h is a compact way to make the callback symbols visible without introducing broad or unrelated includes.
The symbol-map adjustment from it_804DCB68 to @158 indicates the matched output required treating that float constant as compiler-local data instead of an exported global symbol.
The configure.py change was limited to the single object after the source and symbol data were updated, making the matching status explicit and easy to audit.
decomp_lessons: For item files, matching may depend on reconstructing the per-item ItemStateTable with the exact callback ordering and flag values, not only function bodies.
When state callbacks are referenced before or outside their definitions, use the established forward declaration header rather than ad hoc prototypes when that is the project convention.
Do not assume every named sdata2 constant should remain global; matching can require demoting constants to local @NNN labels when the compiler emitted them as local literals.
After an item file is matched, remember to update configure.py from NonMatching to Matching so the build system enforces the new status.
naming_conventions: Item state table symbol used the address-style name it_803F6310, consistent with unresolved or data-symbol naming in the project.
Lipstick callbacks follow the pattern itLipstick_UnkMotionN_{Anim,Phys,Coll}.
The item spawn logic function uses the pattern itLipstick_Logic23_Spawned(Item_GObj* gobj).
Item-specific attributes are accessed through a GET_ATTRS(ip) macro casting xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to itLipstickAttributes*.
Local float constants in symbols.txt may use @NNN labels, as shown by @158 at .sdata2:0x804DCB68.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recreate static data tables exactly: it_803F6310 has six ItemStateTable entries with specific first-field values 0 or 1, callback pointer order, and a NULL collision callback for the third state.
Fix symbol visibility/classification in config/GALE01/symbols.txt when data label scope affects matching; here a global float symbol became a local @158 label.
Use project-level matching configuration as the final indicator: moving itlipstick.c to Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py after source changes.
Evidence is limited to the diff; no PR body or review comments explain the precise mismatch, so the inferred tactic is based on the added table and symbol-map change.
follow_up_queries: Look for nearby item PRs that add ItemStateTable definitions before switching files from NonMatching to Matching.
Search for uses of it/items/forward.h in matched item files to confirm include-order and declaration conventions.
Search symbols.txt changes from it_804* globals to @NNN local labels to identify recurring sdata2 matching cleanup patterns.
Compare itlipstick.c against other item files with UnkMotionN callback tables for shared state table reconstruction patterns.
